input.prompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,

input.initialPrompt:
I need to support three branded themes in my .NET MAUI app — a light theme,
a dark theme, and a high-contrast theme. Users should be able to pick their
preferred theme from a settings page and it should apply immediately across
the app. How do I set this up?

data.content:
Here's the pattern:

### 1. Define each theme as a `ResourceDictionary`

```xml
<!-- Resources/Themes/LightTheme.xaml -->
<ResourceDictionary xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
                    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
                    x:Class="MyApp.Resources.Themes.LightTheme">
    <Color x:Key="PageBackground">#FFFFFF</Color>
    <Color x:Key="PrimaryText">#1A1A1A</Color>
    <Color x:Key="AccentColor">#0078D4</Color>
</ResourceDictionary>
```

Create matching `DarkTheme.xaml` and `HighContrastTheme.xaml` with different color values. Every dictionary must use the **same keys**.

### 2. Load a default theme in `App.xaml`

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <ResourceDictionary>
        <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
            <Resources:LightTheme />
        </ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>
    </ResourceDictionary>
</Application.Resources>
```

### 3. Create a theme-switching helper

```csharp
public enum AppTheme { Light, Dark, HighContrast }

public static class ThemeManager
{
    public static void ApplyTheme(AppTheme theme)
    {
        var mergedDicts = Application.Current!.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
        mergedDicts.Clear();

        mergedDicts.Add(theme switch
        {
            AppTheme.Dark         => new Resources.Themes.DarkTheme(),
            AppTheme.HighContrast => new Resources.Themes.HighContrastTheme(),
            _                     => new Resources.Themes.LightTheme(),
        });

        // Persist the choice
        Preferences.Set("app_theme", theme.ToString());
    }

    public static void LoadSavedTheme()
    {
        if (Enum.TryParse<AppTheme>(Preferences.Get("app_theme", "Light"), out var saved))
            ApplyTheme(saved);
    }
}
```

Call `ThemeManager.LoadSavedTheme()` in your `App` constructor (after `InitializeComponent()`).

### 4. Use `DynamicResource` everywhere

This is the critical part — `StaticResource` won't update at runtime:

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource PageBackground}">
    <Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}"
           Text="Hello, themed world!" />
    <Button BackgroundColor="{DynamicResource AccentColor}"
            Text="Tap me" />
</ContentPage>
```

### 5. Settings page picker

```xml
<Picker x:Name="ThemePicker" Title="Theme"
        SelectedIndexChanged="OnThemeChanged">
    <Picker.ItemsSource>
        <x:Array Type="{x:Type x:String}">
            <x:String>Light</x:String>
            <x:String>Dark</x:String>
            <x:String>High Contrast</x:String>
        </x:Array>
    </Picker.ItemsSource>
</Picker>
```

```csharp
void OnThemeChanged(object sender, EventArgs e)
{
    var theme = ThemePicker.SelectedIndex switch
    {
        1 => AppTheme.Dark,
        2 => AppTheme.HighContrast,
        _ => AppTheme.Light,
    };
    ThemeManager.ApplyTheme(theme);
}
```

### Key points

- **`DynamicResource`** (not `StaticResource`) is what makes the live swap work — it re-evaluates when the dictionary changes.
- **`Preferences`** persists the choice across launches.
- Keep the same keys in every theme dictionary so nothing breaks when you swap.
- For styles (buttons, entries, etc.), define `Style` objects inside each theme dictionary referencing the same color keys, and they'll update too.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for guidance on how to set up themed resource dictionaries in a .NET MAUI app with light, dark, and high-contrast themes, plus a settings page to switch between them at runtime. This is a general knowledge/how-to question, not a codebase task. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
